problemesSolutions.heroSubtitle: Each sector has its specific risks. Discover the protocol adapted to your situation.
problemesSolutions.metaDescription: Sanitary control protocols adapted by sector: livestock, slaughterhouse, and food industry.

elevage.accroche: Intensive livestock farming concentrates all three sanitary risks simultaneously: contaminated surfaces, infected drinking water, air laden with pathogens. The N2K protocol covers all three zones in a mandatory sequence.

steps.title: Surface cleaning
steps.role: Organic biofilm destruction, elimination of fats and proteins. Mandatory surface preparation before disinfection.
steps.constraint: Complete rinsing mandatory before any subsequent step.

steps.title: Mandatory rinsing
steps.role: Drinking water only. Non-optional chemical and regulatory requirement. Chlorinated residues from CLORAGRO are chemically incompatible with OPTIMAGRO.

steps.title: Surface disinfection
steps.role: Full-spectrum biocidal disinfection on rinsed surfaces. Elimination of residual pathogens.
steps.constraint: Minimum 4h ventilation before reintroducing animals. 7-day residual effect.

steps.title: Pipe cleaning and disinfection
steps.role:
Phase 01 — BIONET: shock treatment of drinking circuits. Elimination of scale, biofilm, organic deposits. Dosage 1% to 3% — complete rinsing mandatory after.
Phase 02 — OXYLIS HOCl: continuous introduction in drinking water. Maintenance of microbiological quality throughout the cycle. Dosage 0.1%.

steps.title: Atmosphere treatment
steps.role: Periodic nebulization in the presence of animals. Reduction of airborne microbial load and ammonia concentrations.
steps.constraint: No animal stress, no wetting of litter.

elevage.bioactiveNote: BIOACTIVE is used as a complement to CLORAGRO during sanitary void on equipment with high organic load (dosage 5% to 10%). It is an enzymatic surface cleaner — never in nebulization.

abattoir.accroche: In slaughterhouses, microbial pressure is continuous and cross-contamination risks are at their highest. The N2K protocol covers surface cleaning and disinfection with sector-specific regulatory precautions.

steps.title: Post-production surface cleaning
steps.role: Foam application on floors, walls, equipment, conveyors. Dissolution of animal fats, biofilm destruction.
steps.constraint: Mandatory and abundant rinsing after application.

steps.title: Mandatory rinsing
steps.role: Drinking water only. Non-optional chemical and regulatory requirement before any disinfection.

steps.title: Final disinfection
steps.role: Elimination of residual pathogens — Listeria, Salmonella, E. coli.
steps.constraint: Mandatory rinsing if treated surfaces come into contact with food during the next production. Mandatory ventilation before resuming production.

agroalimentaire.accroche: The food industry requires documented, traceable protocols compliant with HACCP standards. Our products integrate into your existing SMP with complete technical sheets and SDS available on request.

steps.title: Surface cleaning — non-direct food contact
steps.constraint: Do not use on surfaces in direct contact with food without abundant rinsing and analytical validation of residues.

steps.title: Mandatory rinsing
steps.role: Drinking water only. Non-optional chemical and regulatory requirement.

steps.title: Post-cleaning disinfection
steps.constraint: Glutaraldehyde present — mandatory rinsing after application. Never in the presence of food. Mandatory ventilation. Do not use on direct food contact surfaces without prior regulatory validation.

protocol.title: Clean — Sanitize — Maintain
protocol.subtitle: A 3-step protocol for complete sanitary mastery. The sequence of actions is more critical than the products themselves.

step1.label: Pre-cycle
step1.name: Clean
step1.description: Technical cleaning of surfaces: destruction of biofilm, elimination of fats and organic deposits. Essential preparation before disinfection.

step2.label: During cycle
step2.name: Sanitize
step2.description: Full-spectrum biocide disinfection after mandatory rinsing. Stabilization of water and air quality.

step3.label: Continuous maintenance
step3.name: Maintain
step3.description: Continuous monitoring and treatment: water quality, atmosphere, regular microbiological control.

phase1.label: Phase 01
phase1.name: CLORAGRO
features: Radical destruction of Biofilm
Elimination of fats and proteins
Optimal surface preparation

phase2.label: Phase 02
phase2.name: OPTIMAGRO
features: Full spectrum (Bact, Vir, Fung)
Extended persistence 7 days
Protection of new arrivals

batiment.metaTitle: Surface & Floor Hygiene in Livestock — N2K Biosecurity Protocol
batiment.metaDescription: Sequential CLORAGRO + OPTIMAGRO protocol for biofilm destruction and surface disinfection in livestock, slaughterhouses and food industry.
batiment.heroTitle: Zone 01 — The Building: Breaking the pathogen cycle on every surface
batiment.heroSubtitle: Biofilm is an invisible microbial fortress. Disinfection applied directly on biofilm is a wasted investment. Our sequential protocols guarantee a truly sterile surface.

problem.p1: Biofilm is an invisible protective layer produced by bacteria that adheres to livestock building surfaces (floors, equipment, pipes, walls) and acts as a barrier against disinfectants.
problem.p2: When a disinfectant is applied to an intact biofilm, its action often remains superficial, allowing pathogens such as Salmonella, E. coli, or Campylobacter to survive.
problem.p3: This persistent contamination has direct consequences on livestock performance: increased mortality, decreased zootechnical performance, and an increased risk of bacterial resistance.
problem.p4: In Tunisia, heat and humidity conditions accelerate the reformation of biofilm, sometimes in just 24 to 48 hours.

step1.name: CLORAGRO
step1.desc: CLORAGRO is a high-performance alkaline chlorinated detergent designed for the mechanical and chemical destruction of organic biofilm. Its mode of action relies on three simultaneous mechanisms: fat saponification, protein denaturation and destruction of the biofilm polysaccharide matrix.
features: Fat saponification and dissolution of lipid residues
Protein denaturation — destruction of biofilm biological structures
Recommended dilution: 2% to 5% depending on soiling level
Contact time: 15 to 20 minutes — foam or low pressure application
Mandatory PPE: goggles, gloves, boots, coverall

rinse.title: ⚠ MANDATORY RINSING — Critical intermediate step
rinse.desc: After CLORAGRO application, a complete rinse with potable water is MANDATORY before any OPTIMAGRO application. This requirement is chemical: chlorinated residues from CLORAGRO are incompatible with OPTIMAGRO's glutaraldehyde. Direct contact would neutralize both active agents. Rinse all surfaces thoroughly until visual elimination of any residual foam. Check surface pH if necessary (neutral pH required).

step2.name: OPTIMAGRO
step2.desc: OPTIMAGRO combines Glutaraldehyde and Quaternary Ammonium for full-spectrum terminal disinfection. It is applied exclusively on a previously cleaned and rinsed surface. Its action is bactericidal, virucidal and fungicidal with extended residual activity up to 7 days.
features: Full spectrum: Bactericidal, Virucidal, Fungicidal
Targets: Salmonella, E. coli, Newcastle, Gumboro, Aspergillus
Residual activity up to 7 days between flocks
Dilution: 2% — low pressure or spray application
Minimum 4h ventilation before animal reintroduction

kpi.label: Microbial load reduction on treated surfaces
Guaranteed OPTIMAGRO residual activity between flocks
Field validation under real Tunisian conditions

faq.q: Can CLORAGRO be used in the presence of animals?
faq.a: No. CLORAGRO is a corrosive alkaline chlorinated product. It is used exclusively during the sanitary void, in the total absence of animals. Full PPE (goggles, gloves, boots, coverall) is mandatory for the operator.

faq.q: What is the mandatory delay between CLORAGRO and OPTIMAGRO?
faq.a: There is no fixed time delay, but a technical prerequisite: complete and thorough rinsing of all surfaces is mandatory between the two products. Rinse water must be potable. Verify absence of residual foam before applying OPTIMAGRO.

faq.q: Is OPTIMAGRO compatible with stainless steel surfaces?
faq.a: Yes. OPTIMAGRO is compatible with stainless steel, aluminum, PVC, concrete and tiled surfaces. It does not corrode metals at recommended dilutions (2%). Avoid prolonged contact with untreated zinc.

faq.q: How to dose for a 1000 m² building?
faq.a: For CLORAGRO at 2%: plan approximately 200 to 300 liters of solution (200 to 300 mL/m² of floor surface, plus walls). For OPTIMAGRO at 2%: approximately 150 to 200 liters of solution by low pressure spray. These volumes vary according to surface porosity and geometry.

faq.q: Can OPTIMAGRO be combined with other products?
faq.a: No. OPTIMAGRO must never be mixed with other chemical products, particularly chlorinated products (including CLORAGRO), strong acids or iodine-based products. Mixing can generate dangerous chemical reactions and neutralize the biocidal effect.

canalisations.metaTitle: Drinking Water Pipeline Treatment — N2K Biosecurity
canalisations.metaDescription: BIONET + OXYLIS HOCl protocol for cleaning and stabilization of water networks in livestock. Biofilm and scale elimination in pipelines.
canalisations.heroTitle: Zone 02 — Water: The drinking network, primary contamination vector
canalisations.heroSubtitle: An uncontrolled water network transmits pathogens to the entire flock within hours. Control starts at the source and is maintained to the last drinker.

problem.p1: The water contamination cycle in livestock follows a predictable path: reservoir → main pipeline → sub-lines → nipples/drinkers → animal. At each junction point, bacterial biofilm develops in dead zones of the network, elbows, reducers and internal pipe surfaces.
problem.p2: Deposits accumulate in successive layers: limestone scale due to water hardness, bacterial biofilm (E. coli, Pseudomonas, Salmonella), medication and vitamin residues, and organic deposits from the animals themselves. This stratification creates a self-sustaining pathogenic reservoir.
problem.p3: The impact on performance is direct: decreased water consumption (partial obstructions), FCR degradation, increased digestive and respiratory pathologies. An animal drinking contaminated water develops chronic intestinal inflammation that reduces nutrient absorption.
problem.p4: In Tunisia, water hardness (often >30°F) accelerates limestone scale formation. Residual chlorine in the network water is frequently insufficient to maintain acceptable bacteriological quality to the last distribution points.

step1.name: BIONET
step1.desc: BIONET is an agent combining descaler, decruster and detergent for shock cleaning of drinking pipelines. It dissolves limestone deposits, destroys biofilm and eliminates organic residues accumulated in circuits.
features: Triple action: descaler + decruster + detergent
Dissolution of limestone scale and mineral deposits
Biofilm destruction in nipples and drinking circuits
Shock treatment between flocks: concentration 1% to 3%, soaking 2 to 4 hours
Compatible with PVC, polyethylene, stainless steel, rubber gaskets

step2.name: OXYLIS HOCl
step2.desc: OXYLIS HOCl is a bacteriological stabilizer for drinking water. It maintains constant microbiological quality from reservoir to last drinker, throughout the production cycle. Its mode of action controls microbial load without creating resistance.
features: Continuous bacteriological stabilization of drinking water
Automatic dosing: compatible with proportional systems (Dosatron, etc.)
Recommended dosage: 0.1% continuous
No residue in animal tissues at recommended doses
Field monitoring: test strips or rapid kits available

compatibility.title: Compatibility & Safety
items: Compatible with water vaccination (respect 24h delay after BIONET treatment)
Compatible with water-soluble medications and vitamins (check specific interactions)
No residue in animal tissues at recommended doses
BIONET: mandatory and thorough rinsing before restoring drinking water

kpi.label: CFU/mL — Water bacterial load after treatment
Water consumption restored after BIONET descaling
OXYLIS HOCl protection throughout the cycle

faq.q: Can OXYLIS HOCl be used with chicks from Day 1?
faq.a: Yes. OXYLIS HOCl is designed for continuous use from animal placement. The 0.1% dosage is safe for chicks from day one. It is recommended to start treatment before animal arrival to stabilize the network.

faq.q: How to clean heavily scaled nipples?
faq.a: For severe scale, use BIONET at reinforced concentration (3%) with extended soaking of 4 to 6 hours. For heavily scaled individual nipples, disassembly and direct soaking may be necessary. Rinse thoroughly before restoring service.

faq.q: What delay between BIONET and restoring drinking water?
faq.a: After BIONET treatment, rinse the entire circuit thoroughly with potable water. Verify absence of foam and residues. Drinking water can be restored immediately after complete rinsing. Plan 2 to 3 network volumes in rinse water.

faq.q: Can OXYLIS HOCl be used continuously throughout the flock?
faq.a: Yes, this is the recommendation. OXYLIS HOCl is designed for continuous dosing at 0.1% throughout the production cycle. Stopping treatment exposes the network to rapid recontamination, especially in hot climatic conditions.

faq.q: How to calculate dosage for a 20,000 bird farm?
faq.a: Average water consumption is approximately 200 to 250 mL/bird/day in broilers (variable by age and temperature). For 20,000 birds: approximately 4,000 to 5,000 liters/day. At 0.1% OXYLIS HOCl: 4 to 5 liters of pure product per day, via proportional doser.

ambiance.metaTitle: Atmosphere Control & Aero-Biocontamination in Livestock — N2K
ambiance.metaDescription: OXYLIS HOCl + BIOACTIVE protocol for ammonia, dust and airborne microbial load control in livestock buildings.
ambiance.heroTitle: Zone 03 — Atmosphere: Neutralizing the invisible airborne threat
ambiance.heroSubtitle: Ammonia corrodes mucous membranes. Airborne pathogens colonize respiratory tracts before any direct contact. Controlling the atmosphere means protecting the flock at the source.

problem.p1: Livestock building air contains three simultaneous threats: ammonia (NH₃) from manure fermentation, fine particles (litter dust, dander, feathers) and airborne microbial load (bacteria, viruses, fungal spores). These three factors act synergistically to degrade flock respiratory health.
problem.p2: Ammonia attacks respiratory mucous membranes from 15 ppm. Above 25 ppm — a critical threshold documented by multiple scientific studies — ciliated cells of the bronchial epithelium are destroyed. The animal loses its first line of immune defense. Airborne pathogens (Mycoplasma, infectious bronchitis virus, Newcastle) then penetrate without resistance.
problem.p3: The economic consequences are direct: FCR degradation, decreased average daily gain (ADG), increased condemnations at slaughter for respiratory lesions, intercurrent mortality. A degraded atmosphere costs more than preventive treatment.
problem.p4: In Tunisia, conditions worsen the situation: insufficient ventilation in many buildings, wet litter in cold season, high stocking densities. Atmosphere control is often the most neglected link in the sanitary protocol.

step1.name: OXYLIS HOCl
step1.desc: OXYLIS HOCl is a nebulizable solution with airborne biocidal spectrum. Fine suspended particles neutralize pathogens before they reach animal respiratory mucous membranes. Can be used in the presence of livestock without stress or irritation.
features: Use in animal presence — no stress, no irritation
Ammonia (NH₃) reduction: up to -60% in 30 minutes
Airborne pathogen neutralization (bacteria, viruses, spores)
Recommended nebulizer: high pressure, droplets <50 μm
Preventive frequency: 1 to 2 times/day — curative: 3 to 4 times/day

step2.name: BIOACTIVE
step2.desc: BIOACTIVE is a concentrated enzymatic cocktail (proteases, lipases, amylases) that degrades organic matter on surfaces and equipment without friction or chemical vapor. It prepares the environment for OXYLIS HOCl nebulization by reducing emission sources.
features: Triple enzymatic cocktail: proteases, lipases, amylases
Enzymatic degradation without friction or chemical vapor
Surface preparation before OXYLIS HOCl nebulization
Biodegradable — no toxic residue
Foam or spray application on heavily soiled surfaces

equipment.desc: Ceiling-mounted. Covers 200-300 m². Ideal for automated treatments.
Cart-mounted. Flexible, suited for variable-size buildings.
Droplets <50 μm. Better air suspension. Recommended by N2K.
Very fine droplets. More expensive but excellent volumetric coverage.

kpi.label: NH₃ reduction in 30 minutes after OXYLIS HOCl nebulization
Of documented animal stress during OXYLIS HOCl use

faq.q: Is OXYLIS HOCl compatible with spray vaccinations?
faq.a: Do not nebulize OXYLIS HOCl during the 48 hours following a spray or aerosol vaccination. The product could interfere with live attenuated vaccine strains. Resume preventive treatment 48h after vaccination.

faq.q: What type of nebulizer for a 1500 m² building?
faq.a: For a 1500 m² building, plan 5 to 6 static high-pressure nebulizers evenly distributed, or 2 passes with a mobile high-pressure nebulizer. Prefer droplets <50 μm for optimal suspension.

faq.q: Can OXYLIS HOCl and BIOACTIVE be used on the same day?
faq.a: Yes. BIOACTIVE is applied to surfaces (floors, equipment) and OXYLIS HOCl is nebulized in the air. The two products are complementary: BIOACTIVE reduces the source of organic emissions, OXYLIS HOCl treats pathogens already in suspension.

faq.q: How often to nebulize during high heat periods?
faq.a: During high heat periods (>35°C), increase frequency to 3-4 nebulizations per day. Heat accelerates bacterial proliferation and increases ammonia concentrations. Maintain maximum ventilation in parallel.

faq.q: Does OXYLIS HOCl leave residues on eggs or meat?
faq.a: No. At recommended doses (1 mL/m³), OXYLIS HOCl leaves no detectable residues on livestock products. The product acts in gas phase and degrades rapidly. It is designed for use in the presence of production animals.

about.heroSubtitle: More than just application, we deploy hygiene engineering adapted to the real constraints of industrial and intensive farming environments.
about.specialistDesc1: Les Laboratoires N2K stand at the intersection of scientific research and operational efficiency. Based at the Borj Cédria Technopark, we understand the unique challenges of intensive farming in Tunisia.
about.specialistDesc2: Our mission is to break the cycle of contamination by addressing root causes, especially microbial biofilm, often overlooked by standard solutions.

about.manufacturerDesc2: Our approach is built on in-house formulation mastery, a field-oriented methodology, and a concrete understanding of constraints related to hygiene, environment, and industrial applications.
about.manufacturerDesc3: We design solutions engineered to meet the operational realities of the sectors we serve.

about.errorsSubtitle: These common practices drastically reduce the effectiveness of your treatments.

underdosing.title: Systematic under-dosing
underdosing.desc: Believing you save by over-diluting. This promotes microbial resistance and renders treatment ineffective.

noDetergence.title: Neglecting detergence
noDetergence.desc: A disinfectant applied to a dirty surface is instantly neutralized by organic matter.

contactTime.title: Insufficient contact time
contactTime.desc: Rinsing too quickly. Each chemical agent needs 15 to 30 minutes to destroy the cell membrane.

step1.title: Detergence
step1.desc: Application of an alkaline foaming agent. 20 min pause. Grease removal.

step2.title: High Pressure Rinse
step2.desc: Mechanical removal of residues and detergent. The surface must be visually clean.

step3.title: Disinfection
step3.desc: Application by spraying or thermal fogging. Strict compliance with N2K dosage.

cloragro.problem: Resistant biofilms, fats, and encrusted proteins prevent final disinfection from reaching pathogens.
cloragro.where: Poultry farming buildings (Phase 01), Slaughterhouses (Daily), Food industry (non-direct contact).
cloragro.how: Foam application or spraying. Applied first. Equipment: foam cannon, high-pressure washer.
cloragro.why: Foaming alkaline chlorinated formula with corrosion inhibitor. Destroys the biofilm's protective matrix.
cloragro.results: Bare, stripped surfaces, ready for final biocidal disinfection.
cloragro.rinsing: Mandatory and abundant with drinking water before any subsequent step.
cloragro.whenAlone: For maintenance cleaning where microbial pressure is low.
cloragro.whenComplete: Always followed by rinsing then OPTIMAGRO for complete disinfection.
cloragro.whenNot: Never use in the presence of animals or on unrinsed direct contact surfaces.

optimagro.problem: Residual pathogens (Salmonella, Campylobacter, Listeria, viruses) surviving cleaning.
optimagro.where: Poultry farming buildings (Phase 02), Slaughterhouses, Food industry.
optimagro.how: Spraying or nebulization after complete rinsing of surfaces.
optimagro.why: Synergistic action Glutaraldehyde + Quaternary ammoniums. Full-spectrum bactericidal, virucidal, fungicidal.
optimagro.results: Total eradication of pathogens. Extended residual effect up to 7 days.
optimagro.rinsing: Mandatory if surfaces come into contact with food. Incompatible with chlorine.
optimagro.aeration: Minimum 4h before reintroducing animals. Mandatory ventilation in food industry.
optimagro.whenAlone: Only on surfaces previously cleaned and perfectly rinsed.
optimagro.whenComplete: Strictly requires CLORAGRO in Phase 01 for effective action.
optimagro.whenNot: Never apply on chlorinated residues (toxic risk) or in the presence of animals/food.

bionet.problem: Accumulation of scale, mineral deposits, and bacterial biofilm in drinking lines.
bionet.where: Water circuits, pipettes, nipples (Poultry farming only).
bionet.how: Shock treatment (1% to 3%) during sanitary void. Leave to act in pipes.
bionet.why: Dissolves minerals and breaks down internal biofilm. Prevents pipette leaks.
bionet.results: Restored water flow, elimination of internal bacterial nests.
bionet.rinsing: Complete and abundant pressure rinsing of each line is mandatory.
bionet.whenAlone: For periodic descaling during sanitary void.
bionet.whenComplete: To be followed by continuous OXYLIS HOCl when animals are placed.
bionet.whenNot: Never use in the presence of animals. Do not use in slaughterhouses or food industry.

oxylis-hoci.problem: Preserve water lines and improve the atmosphere of livestock and food industry buildings
oxylis-hoci.where: Water networks and environments in farming and food industry.
oxylis-hoci.how: In-line dosing, nebulization or spraying according to maintenance protocol.
oxylis-hoci.why: Versatile technical maintenance solution, compatible with a wide range of applications.
oxylis-hoci.results: Clean, well-maintained installations, simplified operational protocols.
oxylis-hoci.compliance: Compatible with poultry farming and food industry.
oxylis-hoci.whenAlone: For daily maintenance of networks and environments.
oxylis-hoci.whenComplete: Integrates into the complete protocol after BIONET pipe cleaning.

alcosept-pro.problem: Organic residues on work surfaces between production operations.
alcosept-pro.where: Contact surfaces, stainless steel and plastic equipment, packaging areas.
alcosept-pro.how: Direct spraying or manual application between operations.
alcosept-pro.why: Fast-evaporation cleaning for industrial production pace constraints.
alcosept-pro.results: Clean surfaces between operations, residue-free, no extended rinsing.
alcosept-pro.compliance: Suitable for food industry and slaughterhouse environments.
alcosept-pro.whenAlone: For quick intermediate cleaning between production runs.
alcosept-pro.whenComplete: Complement to the complete cleaning protocol.
alcosept-pro.whenNot: Does not replace the full Phase 01 + Phase 02 protocol.

bioactive.problem: Stubborn organic matter (fats, proteins) encrusted on equipment.
bioactive.where: Heavily loaded surfaces and equipment (Poultry farming only).
bioactive.how: Enzymatic cleaning complementing sanitary void (5% to 10%).
bioactive.why: Enzymes specifically digest complex organic matter.
bioactive.results: Perfectly degreased surfaces ready for disinfection.
bioactive.whenComplete: To be used with or after CLORAGRO to optimize surface preparation.
bioactive.whenNot: Never use in nebulization. Do not use in slaughterhouses or food industry.
